page: Vultr vbm-112c-2048gb-8-h100-gpu is a bare metal server configured for high-density parallel computing. It features dual Intel Xeon 8480+ processors with 112 physical cores and 224 vCPUs operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. This bare metal configuration provides direct access to the underlying hardware, eliminating virtualization overhead for intensive workloads.

The server is equipped with eight NVIDIA H100 Hopper GPUs, delivering a combined total of 640 GB of high-bandwidth VRAM. This massive accelerator pool is paired with 2015.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 9.0 GB of RAM per CPU core. This high memory-to-core ratio and massive GPU memory capacity support large-scale model training and complex simulations.

Local storage consists of a 1920 GB NVMe SSD, offering fast read and write speeds for local caching and operating system tasks. The server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. Given its hardware profile, this bare metal instance represents a high-density resource allocation, offering qualitative cost efficiency for organizations requiring dedicated, non-shared GPU clusters without the overhead of public cloud hypervisors.

This configuration is designed for demanding workloads such as large language model training, deep learning inference, high-performance computing simulations, and massive data science pipelines. The combination of eight H100 GPUs and over two terabytes of system RAM makes it suitable for enterprise-grade artificial intelligence and parallel processing tasks.
description: Vultr vbm-112c-2048gb-8-h100-gpu is a high-density bare metal server designed for parallel computing and artificial intelligence workloads. It features 112 physical Intel Xeon 8480+ cores (224 vCPUs) running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, paired with 2015.0 GB of system memory. The server's primary performance driver is its array of eight NVIDIA H100 Hopper GPUs, which provide a total of 640 GB of high-bandwidth VRAM. Local storage is provided by a 1920 GB NVMe SSD, ensuring rapid data access. This dedicated hardware configuration eliminates virtualization overhead, offering high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for large-scale deployments. It is engineered for workloads such as large language model training, deep learning inference, and complex scientific simulations.
og_description: Vultr vbm-112c-2048gb-8-h100-gpu is a bare metal server featuring 224 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 2015 GB of RAM, a 1920 GB NVMe SSD, and eight NVIDIA H100 GPUs with 640 GB of total VRAM for intensive AI workloads.
